review_title: Fast and efficient time series database
description: However, the initial setup and learning the ins and outs of the system can be a bit challenging, but it’s definitely worth it once you’re up and running.
author: Ava H.
pros: I really like how fast and efficient InfluxDB is for handling time-series data. It’s perfect for tracking metrics and events, and querying the data feels quick and easy.
cons: The only thing that bothers me is the steep learning curve. It takes some time to get used to the query language and set up everything properly.

author: Mia G.
pros: I quickly set up a dashboard to monitor server metrics and got real-time insights.
cons: I struggled to find clear instructions on setting up advanced queries.

review_title: InfluxDB - Review from an educational point of view
description: Good as a Time Series database. Ideal to teach students the link between programming, database and visualisations.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: InfluxDB 2.0 is easy in use and understandable for students. Influx is making it possible to store all the data of sensors, energy systems,... easily from software (Python, Node-Red) to visualisation. It is also possible to make visualisations in Influx, but we are using Influx mainly as Timeseries database. Very usefull when working with energy data sets that are time related.
cons: We where struggling in the past when Influx 1.0 went to Influx 2.0. A lot of our research projects had a complete different data structure. So it was a big adjustment. But now when using 2.0. It is better and Influx database became also a lot user friendly then in the past.

review_title: Easy time series storage and analysis
description: I got it to handle a lot of metrics without any issues, the integration with Grafana is nice.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The query language is nice and easy to use
cons: I personally don't like much the ingestion API, so I resorted to use Telegraf with Statsd input.

description: Docker-based implementation on EC2 has been convenient and moving DB to EKS really improved our DB replication and stability.
author: Rakesh M.
pros: Scalability and High-performance are some of the outstanding features of influxdb, we did a stress test of multiple hundreds of IoT clients and scaled effortlessly.
cons: yet another DB with its own language for implementation, text-based searching is not really well implemented and lacks data replication

review_title: Low-code proof of concept for IoT projects
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The ease with which you can connect data from Node red to Influx DB and visualize it with Grafana is seamless and easy to use. It is a perfect platform to create a database for IoT projects and it can handle a lot of data queries without crashing.
cons: It takes a bit of time to get the hang of the flux queries and to convert queries to Flux.

description: used as non-relational DB from Thingworx application, light and fast databases
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: easy to install and deploy also in cluster version
cons: non-relational db are different from normal db so more difficult to learn
